You're available via a neovim plugin that displays a chat window (buffer) that renders markdown for answering questions and helping with workspace files in a git repo.

## Ground rules when writing code:

- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- Do not explain answers, just give me code.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Tool use

Never modify files outside of the current working directory unless requested.

Current directory: ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim

## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`

## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Use `--author="gptoss120b<wes.mcclure+gptoss120b@gmail.com>"`


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.


## Fetch Tool

- Use repo's README `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/evals/refs/heads/main/README.md` instead of `https://github.com/openai/evals`.
- Prefer raw content like `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/g0t4/dotfiles/refs/heads/master/install/symlinks.fish` instead of `https://github.com/g0t4/dotfiles/blob/master/install/symlinks.fish`
- When relevant, prefer github CLI: `gh pr list` or `gh issue view 12`


## Use `apply_patch` to edit files.

Your patch language is a stripped‑down, file‑oriented diff format designed to be easy to parse and safe to apply.

*** Begin Patch
[ one or more file operations ]
*** End Patch

Each “operation” MUST start with one of three “headers”:

*** Add File: <path> - every following line is a + line (the initial contents).
*** Delete File: <path> - remove an existing file. Nothing follows.
*** Update File: <path> - in-place edits (optionally with a rename).

UpdateFile can be immediately followed by MoveTo to rename the file:
*** Move to: <new_path>

Then one or more “hunks”, each introduced by @@ (optionally followed by a hunk header).

Within a hunk each line starts with:
+ for inserted text,
- for removed text, or
  space (" ") for context (unchanged lines to match)

At the end of a truncated hunk you can emit:
*** End of File

### Here is the grammar:

Patch := Begin { FileOp } End
Begin := "*** Begin Patch" NEWLINE
End := "*** End Patch" NEWLINE
FileOp := AddFile | DeleteFile | UpdateFile
AddFile := "*** Add File: " path NEWLINE { "+" line NEWLINE }
DeleteFile := "*** Delete File: " path NEWLINE
UpdateFile := "*** Update File: " path NEWLINE [ MoveTo ] { Hunk }
MoveTo := "*** Move to: " newPath NEWLINE
Hunk := "@@" [ header ] NEWLINE { HunkLine } [ "*** End of File" NEWLINE ]
HunkLine := (" " | "-" | "+") text NEWLINE

### A full patch can combine several operations:

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: hello.txt
+Hello world
*** Update File: src/app.py
*** Move to: src/main.py
@@ def greet():
-print("Hi")
+print("Hello, world!")
*** Delete File: obsolete.txt
*** End Patch

### Reminders

- Verify your changes with `git diff foo.json`
- Nothing wrong with commands too:
  - `rm foo.json` to delete
  - `mv foo.json bar.json` to rename
  - `rename` for bulk rename
- Don't be that guy that uses DeleteFile followed by AddFile just to rename a file!


Tips on a failure:
- You only need enough context for a unique match!
- The line(s) you replace are context too; if they’re unique, no additional context is needed.
- Split bigger patches into smaller patches, i.e. if editing two functions, try each separately!
- Watch out for whitespace differences, i.e. blank lines

Please be curteous:
- Check your work!

### More Examples

Here's a patch file to change the `win` property's type:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@ @dataclass
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
-    gap_rms: TimeSeries
-    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
+    gap_rms: TimeSeries
+    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
*** End Patch
```

Which can be simplified to:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

Or, this could work if the `win` property is unique:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-    win: GapWindow
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

in multiline scenarios\n-- will split on and emit events when the blank line is detected\n-- will have a clean interface for consumers to subscribe to events/done/etc\n-- this will plug into my curl module (and other chat completion endpoint clients)\n\n-- * FORMAT:\n-- https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#event_stream_format\n-- ^: == comment (often used as keep-alive)\n-- UTF-8\n-- mimetype: https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/iana.html#text/event-stream\n-- SPEC:\n--   https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html#parsing-an-event-stream\n--\n-- event (aka message)\n--   separated by a pair of newline characters\n--   delimited by blank line ==> \\n\\n\n--   have field(s)\n-- data field\n--   multiple data lines are concatenated\n--   thus far I have only observed data-only events (IOTW no other fields)\n--\n-- browser event dispatch:\n--   addEventListener() for named (typed) events\n--   onmessage() when no type\n\n---@alias SSEOnDataHandler function(data string)\n\n---Think of this as unwrapping the SSE envelope, extracting events and triggering handlers\n---for each event type, though in this case data is the only event needed (thus far)\n---\n---This should work for more than just OpenAI endpoints... any data only SSE endpoint\n---\n---@class SSEDataOnlyParser\n---@field _buffer string\n---@field _done boolean\n---@field _on_data_sse SSEOnDataHandler\nlocal SSEDataOnlyParser = {}\n\n---@param on_data_sse SSEOnDataHandler\n---@return SSEDataOnlyParser\nfunction SSEDataOnlyParser.new(on_data_sse)\n    local instance = setmetatable({}, { __index = SSEDataOnlyParser })\n    instance._buffer = \"\"\n    instance._done = false\n    instance._on_data_sse = on_data_sse\n    return instance\nend\n\n---@return string? invalid_dreg -- if the buffer has leftover, invalid JSON, return an error describing it\nfunction SSEDataOnlyParser:flush_dregs()\n    local no_dregs = not self._buffer or self._buffer == \"\"\n    if no_dregs then\n        return nil\n    end\n\n    local success, sse_parsed = safely.decode_json(self._buffer)\n    if not success then\n        return \"invalid json in dregs: \" .. self._buffer\n    end\n\n    -- PRN? just flush it to _on_data_sse every time and let that blow up\n    -- (it has error handler in it)\n    --   if I want terminate behavior then this would be wise\n\n    -- YES I know this means the parsed JSON object is discarded and re-parsed in:\n    self._on_data_sse(self._buffer)\nend\n\n--- curl stdout should be patched into this\n---@param data string -- NOTE data is not synonymous with line/field/event (it can be any one of these, or NONE of these)\nfunction SSEDataOnlyParser:write(data)\n    -- log:info(\"data\", vim.inspect(data))\n\n    -- normalize line endings to LF\n    data = data:gsub(\"\\r\\n\", \"\\n\"):gsub(\"\\r\", \"\\n\")\n\n    -- simple idea... BUFFER writes until we have an event separator (\\n\\n)\n    self._buffer = self._buffer .. data\n\n    -- * blank line signals end of event\n    -- *** \\n\\n is EVENT SEPARATOR\n    -- FYI split takes plain and trimempty options\n    --   default not removing empties, can use to check if a \\n\\n was present\n    local events = vim.split(self._buffer, \"\\n\\n\", {})\n\n    if (#events == 1) then\n        -- no event separator (blank line) yet\n        return\n    elseif (#events >= 2) then\n        -- found event separator(s)!\n\n        -- ==> emit completed data event(s)\n        for i = 1, #events - 1 do\n            local event = events[i]\n\n            -- SSEs (events) are comprised of \\n delimited fields\n            -- *** \\n is FIELD SEPARATOR (cannot have another \\n next to it)\n            local fields = vim.split(event, \"\\n\")\n\n            local data_value = vim.iter(fields)\n                :filter(function(f)\n                    -- limit to data fields, ignore the rest for now\n                    return f:match(\"^data:\")\n                end)\n                :map(function(f)\n                    -- strip data: prefix on each data field\n                    -- PRN later can handle or parse or otherwise split the field name and value (i.e. for event: message)\n                    local result, _ = f:gsub(\"^data: \", \"\")\n                    return result\n                end)\n                :join(\"\\n\")\n\n            local is_whitespace_only = data_value:match(\"^%s*$\")\n            if is_whitespace_only then\n                log:trace(\"skipping empty SSE: \" .. vim.inspect(data_value))\n                return\n            end\n            self._on_data_sse(data_value)\n        end\n\n        -- keep last line in buffer (it's not complete w/o a blank line)\n        self._buffer = events[#events]\n    end\nend\n\n--- TESTING ONLY - You're available via a neovim plugin that displays a chat window (buffer) that renders markdown for answering questions and helping with workspace files in a git repo.

## Ground rules when writing code:

- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- Do not explain answers, just give me code.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Tool use

Never modify files outside of the current working directory unless requested.

Current directory: ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim

## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`

## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Use `--author="gptoss120b<wes.mcclure+gptoss120b@gmail.com>"`


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.


## Fetch Tool

- Use repo's README `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/evals/refs/heads/main/README.md` instead of `https://github.com/openai/evals`.
- Prefer raw content like `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/g0t4/dotfiles/refs/heads/master/install/symlinks.fish` instead of `https://github.com/g0t4/dotfiles/blob/master/install/symlinks.fish`
- When relevant, prefer github CLI: `gh pr list` or `gh issue view 12`


## Use `apply_patch` to edit files.

Your patch language is a stripped‑down, file‑oriented diff format designed to be easy to parse and safe to apply.

*** Begin Patch
[ one or more file operations ]
*** End Patch

Each “operation” MUST start with one of three “headers”:

*** Add File: <path> - every following line is a + line (the initial contents).
*** Delete File: <path> - remove an existing file. Nothing follows.
*** Update File: <path> - in-place edits (optionally with a rename).

UpdateFile can be immediately followed by MoveTo to rename the file:
*** Move to: <new_path>

Then one or more “hunks”, each introduced by @@ (optionally followed by a hunk header).

Within a hunk each line starts with:
+ for inserted text,
- for removed text, or
  space (" ") for context (unchanged lines to match)

At the end of a truncated hunk you can emit:
*** End of File

### Here is the grammar:

Patch := Begin { FileOp } End
Begin := "*** Begin Patch" NEWLINE
End := "*** End Patch" NEWLINE
FileOp := AddFile | DeleteFile | UpdateFile
AddFile := "*** Add File: " path NEWLINE { "+" line NEWLINE }
DeleteFile := "*** Delete File: " path NEWLINE
UpdateFile := "*** Update File: " path NEWLINE [ MoveTo ] { Hunk }
MoveTo := "*** Move to: " newPath NEWLINE
Hunk := "@@" [ header ] NEWLINE { HunkLine } [ "*** End of File" NEWLINE ]
HunkLine := (" " | "-" | "+") text NEWLINE

### A full patch can combine several operations:

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: hello.txt
+Hello world
*** Update File: src/app.py
*** Move to: src/main.py
@@ def greet():
-print("Hi")
+print("Hello, world!")
*** Delete File: obsolete.txt
*** End Patch

### Reminders

- Verify your changes with `git diff foo.json`
- Nothing wrong with commands too:
  - `rm foo.json` to delete
  - `mv foo.json bar.json` to rename
  - `rename` for bulk rename
- Don't be that guy that uses DeleteFile followed by AddFile just to rename a file!


Tips on a failure:
- You only need enough context for a unique match!
- The line(s) you replace are context too; if they’re unique, no additional context is needed.
- Split bigger patches into smaller patches, i.e. if editing two functions, try each separately!
- Watch out for whitespace differences, i.e. blank lines

Please be curteous:
- Check your work!

### More Examples

Here's a patch file to change the `win` property's type:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@ @dataclass
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
-    gap_rms: TimeSeries
-    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
+    gap_rms: TimeSeries
+    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
*** End Patch
```

Which can be simplified to:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

Or, this could work if the `win` property is unique:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-    win: GapWindow
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

## General Code Preferences

- When rewriting code, leave unrelated code and unrelated comments as is.
- When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example:
```lua
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## Lua Code Preferences

Naming
- Use snake_case for functions and variables.
- Use PascalCase for classes.

Type Hints
- Prefer EmmyLua/LuaLS style annotations
- Always annotate when type inference would be ambiguous, or for public APIs.

Examples:
```lua
---@param names string[]
---@param checker fun(name: string): boolean
---@return table<string, integer>
function process_names(names, checker) end

---@class WindowController
---@field window hs.axuielement
---@field title? string
---@field size { w: number, h: number }
local WindowController = {}
```

```lua/ask-openai/backends/sse/data_only_parser.tests.lua

require("ask-openai.helpers.test_setup").modify_package_path()
local SSEDataOnlyParser = require("ask-openai.backends.sse.data_only_parser")
local describe = require("devtools.tests._describe")

only = it
-- it = ignore

describe("data-only events", function()
    local events = {}
    local function on_data_sse(data)
        table.insert(events, data)
    end
    local parser
    before_each(function()
        events = {}
        parser = SSEDataOnlyParser.new(on_data_sse)
    end)

    local function escape_newlines(s)
        -- this is for display purposes in test description headers
        return s:gsub("\n", "\\n"):gsub("\r", "\\r")
    end

    local line_ending_types = { "\n", "\r\n", "\r" }
    for _, line_ending in ipairs(line_ending_types) do
        local blank_line = line_ending .. line_ending
        describe(escape_newlines(blank_line), function()
            -- FYI I am ONLY testing this one case with \r and \r\n endings... I don't really think I will ever encounter this
            --  and I don't want to muck up my LF ending tests that make sense to me and should be all I ever see on *nix anyways
            --  FTR it would be fine to strip out the \r and \r\n handling entirely

            describe("single data value in single write", function()
                before_each(function()
                    parser:write('data: data_value1' .. blank_line)
                end)

                it("should emit event", function()
                    assert.are.same({ "data_value1" }, events)
                end)
            end)
        end)
    end

    describe("concatenate", function()
        only("two data fields in the same event => joined with \\n", function()
            parser:writes {
                -- FYI you must have \n at the end of a field to delimit it from other fields in the same event, including with multiple data field values
                "data: hello\n", -- *** \n is FIELD SEPARATOR (cannot have another \n next to it)
                "data: world\n\n", -- *** \n\n is EVENT SEPARATOR
            }
            assert.are.same({ "hello\nworld" }, events)
        end)
        it("non-consecutive data fields are ALSO concatenated in order", function()
            parser:writes {
                "data: val1\n", -- data field
                "event: message\n", -- event field
                "data: val2\n\n", -- data field
            }
            assert.are.same({ "val1\nval2" }, events)
        end)
        -- edge cases:
        it("two writes IS NOT two fields", function()
            -- *** THERE IS NO IMPLICIT \n after each write!
            parser:writes {
                "data: hello", -- no \n means field continues on the next write
                "data: world\n\n",
            }
            assert.are.same({ "hellodata: world" }, events)
        end)
        it("data value split across two writes", function()
            parser:writes {
                "data: data_va", -- again, no \n means field continues on the next write
                "lue1\n\n"
            }
            assert.are.same({ "data_value1" }, events)
        end)
    end)

    it("multiple events in single write", function()
        parser:writes {
            -- NOTE \n\n == Event Separator (must have one after each event)
            "data: hello\n\ndata: world\n\n"
        }
        assert.are.same({ "hello", "world" }, events)
    end)

    it("'data: ' is at the start of a new event's data value", function()
        -- edge case to make sure not replacing multiple `data:` and/or not iteratively replacing one at a time (i.e. in a loop)
        local write = "data: data: \n\n"
        parser:write(write)
        assert.are.same({ "data: " }, events)
    end)

    describe("dregs with no trailing blank line", function()
        describe("dregs is valid JSON", function()
            -- (via llama server error object when no --jinja flag and try to use tools)
            local llama_server_error_no_end_newlines = [[{"error":{"code":500,"message":"tools param requires --jinja flag","type":"server_error"}}]]
            -- ALSO no `data: ` prefix - DO NOT test that here too (split it apart if you want that test case to use this scenario too)

            -- PRN! add flush_dregs to an integration test?
            --   manual testing: remove --jinja, try using tools!

            it("only one newline at end => treats as SSE", function()
                local write1 = llama_server_error_no_end_newlines .. "\n"
                parser:write(write1)

                local error_text = parser:flush_dregs() -- intended to be called in on_exit assert.are.same({ write1 }, events)

                assert.are.same({ write1 }, events)
                assert.are.same(nil, error_text)
            end)

            it("NO newline at end => treats as SSE", function()
                local write1 = llama_server_error_no_end_newlines
                parser:write(write1)

                local error_text = parser:flush_dregs()

                assert.are.same({ write1 }, events)
                assert.are.same(nil, error_text)
            end)
        end)

        describe("dregs is not valid JSON object", function()
            local not_valid_json = "notvalidjson"

            it("only one newline at end => logs warning", function()
```

{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"0","name":"EXIT_CODE"},{"type":"text","text":"local log = require("ask-openai.logs.logger").predictions()
local safely = require("ask-openai.helpers.safely")

-- stream parser will sit BELOW a chat completions client
-- this will buffer data fields in multiline scenarios
-- will split on and emit events when the blank line is detected
-- will have a clean interface for consumers to subscribe to events/done/etc
-- this will plug into my curl module (and other chat completion endpoint clients)

-- * FORMAT:
-- https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#event_stream_format
-- ^: == comment (often used as keep-alive)
-- UTF-8
-- mimetype: https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/iana.html#text/event-stream
-- SPEC:
--   https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html#parsing-an-event-stream
--
-- event (aka message)
--   separated by a pair of newline characters
--   delimited by blank line ==> \n\n
--   have field(s)
-- data field
--   multiple data lines are concatenated
--   thus far I have only observed data-only events (IOTW no other fields)
--
-- browser event dispatch:
--   addEventListener() for named (typed) events
--   onmessage() when no type

---@alias SSEOnDataHandler function(data string)

---Think of this as unwrapping the SSE envelope, extracting events and triggering handlers
---for each event type, though in this case data is the only event needed (thus far)
---
---This should work for more than just OpenAI endpoints... any data only SSE endpoint
---
---@class SSEDataOnlyParser
---@field _buffer string
---@field _done boolean
---@field _on_data_sse SSEOnDataHandler
local SSEDataOnlyParser = {}

---@param on_data_sse SSEOnDataHandler
---@return SSEDataOnlyParser
function SSEDataOnlyParser.new(on_data_sse)
    local instance = setmetatable({}, { __index = SSEDataOnlyParser })
    instance._buffer = ""
    instance._done = false
    instance._on_data_sse = on_data_sse
    return instance
end

---@return string? invalid_dreg -- if the buffer has leftover, invalid JSON, return an error describing it
function SSEDataOnlyParser:flush_dregs()
    local no_dregs = not self._buffer or self._buffer == ""
    if no_dregs then
        return nil
    end

    local success, sse_parsed = safely.decode_json(self._buffer)
    if not success then
        return "invalid json in dregs: " .. self._buffer
    end

    -- PRN? just flush it to _on_data_sse every time and let that blow up
    -- (it has error handler in it)
    --   if I want terminate behavior then this would be wise

    -- YES I know this means the parsed JSON object is discarded and re-parsed in:
    self._on_data_sse(self._buffer)
end

--- curl stdout should be patched into this
---@param data string -- NOTE data is not synonymous with line/field/event (it can be any one of these, or NONE of these)
function SSEDataOnlyParser:write(data)
    -- log:info("data", vim.inspect(data))

    -- normalize line endings to LF
    data = data:gsub("\r\n", "\n"):gsub("\r", "\n")

    -- simple idea... BUFFER writes until we have an event separator (\n\n)
    self._buffer = self._buffer .. data

    -- * blank line signals end of event
    -- *** \n\n is EVENT SEPARATOR
    -- FYI split takes plain and trimempty options
    --   default not removing empties, can use to check if a \n\n was present
    local events = vim.split(self._buffer, "\n\n", {})

    if (#events == 1) then
        -- no event separator (blank line) yet
        return
    elseif (#events >= 2) then
        -- found event separator(s)!

        -- ==> emit completed data event(s)
        for i = 1, #events - 1 do
            local event = events[i]

            -- SSEs (events) are comprised of \n delimited fields
            -- *** \n is FIELD SEPARATOR (cannot have another \n next to it)
            local fields = vim.split(event, "\n")

            local data_value = vim.iter(fields)
                :filter(function(f)
                    -- limit to data fields, ignore the rest for now
                    return f:match("^data:")
                end)
                :map(function(f)
                    -- strip data: prefix on each data field
                    -- PRN later can handle or parse or otherwise split the field name and value (i.e. for event: message)
                    local result, _ = f:gsub("^data: ", "")
                    return result
                end)
                :join("\n")
            self._on_data_sse(data_value)
        end

        -- keep last line in buffer (it's not complete w/o a blank line)
        self._buffer = events[#events]
    end
end

--- TESTING ONLY - Write multiple chunks sequentially.
---@param writes string[] List of data chunks to write.
function SSEDataOnlyParser:writes(writes)
    for _, chunk in ipairs(writes) do
        self:write(chunk)
    end
end

return SSEDataOnlyParser

{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"0","name":"EXIT_CODE"},{"type":"text","text":"local log = require("ask-openai.logs.logger").predictions()
local safely = require("ask-openai.helpers.safely")

-- stream parser will sit BELOW a chat completions client
-- this will buffer data fields in multiline scenarios
-- will split on and emit events when the blank line is detected
-- will have a clean interface for consumers to subscribe to events/done/etc
-- this will plug into my curl module (and other chat completion endpoint clients)

-- * FORMAT:
-- https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#event_stream_format
-- ^: == comment (often used as keep-alive)
-- UTF-8
-- mimetype: https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/iana.html#text/event-stream
-- SPEC:
--   https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html#parsing-an-event-stream
--
-- event (aka message)
--   separated by a pair of newline characters
--   delimited by blank line ==> \n\n
--   have field(s)
-- data field
--   multiple data lines are concatenated
--   thus far I have only observed data-only events (IOTW no other fields)
--
-- browser event dispatch:
--   addEventListener() for named (typed) events
--   onmessage() when no type

---@alias SSEOnDataHandler function(data string)

---Think of this as unwrapping the SSE envelope, extracting events and triggering handlers
---for each event type, though in this case data is the only event needed (thus far)
---
---This should work for more than just OpenAI endpoints... any data only SSE endpoint
---
---@class SSEDataOnlyParser
---@field _buffer string
---@field _done boolean
---@field _on_data_sse SSEOnDataHandler
local SSEDataOnlyParser = {}

---@param on_data_sse SSEOnDataHandler
---@return SSEDataOnlyParser
function SSEDataOnlyParser.new(on_data_sse)
    local instance = setmetatable({}, { __index = SSEDataOnlyParser })
    instance._buffer = ""
    instance._done = false
    instance._on_data_sse = on_data_sse
    return instance
end

---@return string? invalid_dreg -- if the buffer has leftover, invalid JSON, return an error describing it
function SSEDataOnlyParser:flush_dregs()
    local no_dregs = not self._buffer or self._buffer == ""
    if no_dregs then
        return nil
    end

    local success, sse_parsed = safely.decode_json(self._buffer)
    if not success then
        return "invalid json in dregs: " .. self._buffer
    end

    -- PRN? just flush it to _on_data_sse every time and let that blow up
    -- (it has error handler in it)
    --   if I want terminate behavior then this would be wise

    -- YES I know this means the parsed JSON object is discarded and re-parsed in:
    self._on_data_sse(self._buffer)
end

--- curl stdout should be patched into this
---@param data string -- NOTE data is not synonymous with line/field/event (it can be any one of these, or NONE of these)
function SSEDataOnlyParser:write(data)
    -- log:info("data", vim.inspect(data))

    -- normalize line endings to LF
    data = data:gsub("\r\n", "\n"):gsub("\r", "\n")

    -- simple idea... BUFFER writes until we have an event separator (\n\n)
    self._buffer = self._buffer .. data

    -- * blank line signals end of event
    -- *** \n\n is EVENT SEPARATOR
    -- FYI split takes plain and trimempty options
    --   default not removing empties, can use to check if a \n\n was present
    local events = vim.split(self._buffer, "\n\n", {})

    if (#events == 1) then
        -- no event separator (blank line) yet
        return
    elseif (#events >= 2) then
        -- found event separator(s)!

        -- ==> emit completed data event(s)
        for i = 1, #events - 1 do
            local event = events[i]

            -- SSEs (events) are comprised of \n delimited fields
            -- *** \n is FIELD SEPARATOR (cannot have another \n next to it)
            local fields = vim.split(event, "\n")

            local data_value = vim.iter(fields)
                :filter(function(f)
                    -- limit to data fields, ignore the rest for now
                    return f:match("^data:")
                end)
                :map(function(f)
                    -- strip data: prefix on each data field
                    -- PRN later can handle or parse or otherwise split the field name and value (i.e. for event: message)
                    local result, _ = f:gsub("^data: ", "")
                    return result
                end)
                :join("\n")

            local is_whitespace_only = data_value:match("^%s*$")
            if is_whitespace_only then
                log:trace("skipping empty SSE: " .. vim.inspect(data_value))
                return
            end
            self._on_data_sse(data_value)
        end

        -- keep last line in buffer (it's not complete w/o a blank line)
        self._buffer = events[#events]
    end
end

--- TESTING ONLY - Write multiple chunks sequentially.
---@param writes string[] List of data chunks to write.
function SSEDataOnlyParser:writes(writes)
    for _, chunk in ipairs(writes) do
        self:write(chunk)
    end
end

return SSEDataOnlyParser
